what_it_guards: The contract across the shared artifact layer (the [FEATURE]-IDEAS.md Page states tree): code-agent must refuse + route when the tree is missing/thin/draft-only, and proceed when it is locked+adequate; spec-research must produce a DRAFT-marked tree in the exact template columns (never code) and mirror legacy branches for ports.
fixture_model: Each case composes the shared base/ project with exactly one state overlay copied to docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md (port cases also copy ports/HabitTrackerLegacy.jsx to _port_source/). The state overlay is the only variable — diff it to see what the agent was given.
grading: Each expectation is graded pass/fail against the transcript + the post-run workdir (files-modified). Record evidence per expectation, matching skills-workspace/iteration-*/benchmark.json shape.

cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Follow the project's CLAUDE.md and conventions. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Refuses to implement; writes no source files
Cites the missing ## Page states section specifically (not a generic 'spec unclear')
Routes to /spec-research HABIT-TRACKER (not /design-explore or /design-agent — direction is locked, DESIGN-HEURISTICS.md present)
Does NOT silently code the happy path

cases.behavior: 2 — Page states present but inadequate → still refuse
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Follow the project's CLAUDE.md and conventions. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Refuses despite a ## Page states table being present (adequacy gate, not just existence)
Names the gap concretely: API in src/lib/api/habits.ts implies 401/403/409/500 + empty, table lists only loading+success
Flags decision #4 'handle the tier-gated case gracefully' as a vague verb lacking a render rule
Routes to /spec-research rather than critiquing the doc inline itself
expectations.evidence_hint: does not rewrite the table in-skill

cases.behavior: 3 — DRAFT present but unlocked → must still refuse
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Follow the project's CLAUDE.md and conventions. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Refuses: recognizes the Page states rows are Status: proposed and wrapped in DRAFT markers, i.e. not yet locked
Does NOT treat the draft as spec and implement against the proposed rows
Asks the user to review and lock the draft (remove DRAFT markers / set Status: locked) before proceeding
Does not re-run research redundantly — points at the existing draft rather than routing back to /spec-research to regenerate it

cases.behavior: 4 — locked + adequate → proceeds, no spurious route
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Follow the project's CLAUDE.md and conventions. Produce your phased implementation plan and begin phase 1; you may stop after phase 1 is scaffolded.

expectations.text: Proceeds — does NOT route to /spec-research (the gate must not be over-eager on a complete spec)
expectations.evidence_hint: no refusal; no spec-research mention as a blocker

expectations.text: Plan covers all 7 locked states, not just the happy path
Uses tokens from DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md (CSS custom props), no raw hex/px literals
Routes API calls through src/lib/api/, components don't fetch directly (CLAUDE.md convention)

cases.behavior: 1/2 (port) — thin port spec → refuse + route citing source reconciliation
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to port the Habit Tracker page from _port_source/HabitTrackerLegacy.jsx into this app, following docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Follow the project's CLAUDE.md and conventions. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Refuses; writes no source files
Notices the legacy source has ~7 render branches while the Page states table lists 2
Routes to /spec-research for the port reconciliation (Source mirror) rather than porting the 2 listed states and dropping the rest
Does not silently carry legacy baggage (e.g. Redux patterns) into the SvelteKit target

cases.behavior: 5 — produces DRAFT-marked Page states in exact template columns, never code
cases.prompt: Use the /spec-research skill on docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Read the data contract in src/lib/api/habits.ts and the project docs, then propose the decision tree. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Writes a ## Page states table using the template columns: # | State | Trigger | Renders | Dispatches | Exit | Status
Output is wrapped in a DRAFT marker comment and rows are Status: proposed (not locked unilaterally)
Writes NO source files — markdown proposal only
Derives states from the API contract: loading, empty, success, already-done(409), tier-limit(403), unauthenticated(401), error(500)

cases.behavior: 5+7 — surfaces adjacent gaps and returns a clean resumption hint
cases.prompt: Use the /spec-research skill on docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md as if invoked by /code-agent's preflight router. Read the data contract and docs, propose the tree, and report back to the caller. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Produces an ## Adjacent gaps section with questions for the user (not unilateral answers)
Surfaces at least one real gap, e.g. free-tier habit cap (session.freeHabitLimit) vs 403 behavior, or empty-state CTA copy
Returns a structured handoff report: where the draft was written + a one-line 'once locked, return to /code-agent' hint
Does not echo the full draft back to the caller — points at it
expectations.evidence_hint: report is a pointer, not a paste

cases.behavior: 6 — port: mirror legacy render branches, surface missing states
cases.prompt: Use the /spec-research skill on docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. This is a port; the legacy source is at _port_source/HabitTrackerLegacy.jsx. Reconcile the spec against the source. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Produces a ## Source mirror block anchored above Page states (port-only structure)
Mirrors all ~7 legacy branches (loading, 401 redirect, 403 upgrade+readonly list, 500 error, empty, success, 409 already-done) to target states
Flags the 5 missing states absent from the current 2-row table as the reconciliation gap
Surfaces the premium-gated reorder affordance (useFlag('multi_habit')) and maps it to the session tier check per REPO-CONVENTIONS rule #3
Writes no source files

cases.behavior: 5 — augments a partial table rather than rewriting from scratch
cases.prompt: Use the /spec-research skill on docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. The Page states table is partial. Propose what's missing. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Preserves the 2 existing rows and proposes the missing ones as DRAFT additions (does not discard the user's locked rows)
Proposes the missing branches: empty, 401, 403, 409, 500
Flags decision #4 'handle the tier-gated case gracefully' as a vague verb needing a concrete render rule
Writes no source files

cases.behavior: guard — already-adequate spec → no redundant rewrite
cases.prompt: Use the /spec-research skill on docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md. Do whatever the skill says to do.

expectations.text: Recognizes the Page states tree is already complete and locked (7 states, no vague verbs)
Does NOT unilaterally rewrite or re-propose the already-locked rows as a fresh draft
Either reports 'spec is adequate, nothing to research' or surfaces only genuinely new gaps — does not manufacture work
